content.text: I found the oxlint output formatter files and existing GitHub formatter snapshots; next I’ll inspect the relevant formatter and lint command code to see where summary metadata is available.

content.content:
1	use std::path::PathBuf;
2	
3	use bpaf::Bpaf;
4	use oxc_linter::{AllowWarnDeny, FixKind, LintPlugins};
5	
6	use crate::output_formatter::OutputFormat;
7	
8	use super::{
9	    MiscOptions, PATHS_ERROR_MESSAGE, VERSION,
10	    ignore::{IgnoreOptions, ignore_options},
11	    misc_options, validate_paths,
12	};
13	
14	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Bpaf)]
15	#[bpaf(options, version(VERSION))]
16	pub struct LintCommand {
17	    #[bpaf(external)]
18	    pub basic_options: BasicOptions,
19	
20	    #[bpaf(external(lint_filter), map(LintFilter::into_tuple), many, hide_usage)]
21	    pub filter: Vec<(AllowWarnDeny, String)>,
22	
23	    #[bpaf(external)]
24	    pub enable_plugins: EnablePlugins,
25	
26	    #[bpaf(external)]
27	    pub fix_options: FixOptions,
28	
29	    #[bpaf(external)]
30	    pub ignore_options: IgnoreOptions,
31	
32	    #[bpaf(external)]
33	    pub warning_options: WarningOptions,
34	
35	    #[bpaf(external)]
36	    pub output_options: OutputOptions,
37	
38	    /// List all the rules that are currently registered
39	    #[bpaf(long("rules"), switch, hide_usage)]
40	    pub list_rules: bool,
41	
42	    /// Start the language server
43	    #[bpaf(long("lsp"), switch, hide_usage)]
44	    pub lsp: bool,
45	
46	    #[bpaf(external)]
47	    pub misc_options: MiscOptions,
48	
49	    /// Disable the automatic loading of nested configuration files
50	    #[bpaf(switch, hide_usage)]
51	    pub disable_nested_config: bool,
52	
53	    /// Enable rules that require type information
54	    #[bpaf(switch, hide_usage)]
55	    pub type_aware: bool,
56	
57	    /// Enable experimental type checking (includes TypeScript compiler diagnostics)
58	    #[bpaf(switch, hide_usage)]
59	    pub type_check: bool,
60	
61	    /// Run only TypeScript type checking diagnostics without regular lint diagnostics
62	    #[bpaf(long("type-check-only"), switch, hide)]
63	    pub type_check_only: bool,
64	
65	    #[bpaf(external)]
66	    pub inline_config_options: InlineConfigOptions,
67	
68	    /// Single file, single path or list of paths
69	    #[bpaf(positional("PATH"), many, guard(validate_paths, PATHS_ERROR_MESSAGE))]
70	    pub paths: Vec<PathBuf>,
71	}
72	
73	impl LintCommand {
74	    pub fn handle_threads(&self) {
75	        Self::init_rayon_thread_pool(self.misc_options.threads);
76	    }
77	
78	    /// Initialize Rayon global thread pool with specified number of threads.
79	    ///
80	    /// If `--threads` option is not used, or `--threads 0` is given,
81	    /// default to the number of available CPU cores.
82	    #[expect(clippy::print_stderr)]
83	    fn init_rayon_thread_pool(threads: Option<usize>) {
84	        // Always initialize thread pool, even if using default thread count,
85	        // to ensure thread pool's thread count is locked after this point.
86	        // `rayon::current_num_threads()` will always return the same number after this point.
87	        //
88	        // If you don't initialize the global thread pool explicitly, or don't specify `num_threads`,
89	        // Rayon will initialize the thread pool when it's first used, with a thread count of
90	        // `std::thread::available_parallelism()`, and that thread count won't change thereafter.
91	        // So we don't *need* to initialize the thread pool here if we just want the default thread count.
92	        //
93	        // However, Rayon's docs state that:
94	        // > In the future, the default behavior may change to dynamically add or remove threads as needed.
95	        // https://docs.rs/rayon/1.11.0/rayon/struct.ThreadPoolBuilder.html#method.num_threads
96	        //
97	        // To ensure we continue to have a "locked" thread count, even after future Rayon upgrades,
98	        // we always initialize the thread pool and explicitly specify thread count here.
99	
100	        let thread_count = if let Some(thread_count) = threads
101	            && thread_count > 0
102	        {
103	            thread_count
104	        } else if let Ok(thread_count) = std::thread::available_parallelism() {
105	            thread_count.get()
106	        } else {
107	            eprintln!(
108	                "Unable to determine available thread count. Defaulting to 1.\nConsider specifying the number of threads explicitly with `--threads` option."
109	            );
110	            1
111	        };
112	
113	        rayon::ThreadPoolBuilder::new().num_threads(thread_count).build_global().unwrap();
114	    }
115	}
116	
117	/// Basic Configuration
118	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Bpaf)]
119	pub struct BasicOptions {
120	    /// Oxlint configuration file
121	    ///  * `.json` and `.jsonc` config files are supported in all runtimes
122	    ///  * JavaScript/TypeScript config files are experimental and require running via Node.js
123	    ///  * you can use comments in configuration files.
124	    ///  * tries to be compatible with ESLint v8's format
125	    ///
126	    /// If not provided, Oxlint will look for a `.oxlintrc.json`, `.oxlintrc.jsonc`, or `oxlint.config.ts` file in the current working directory.
127	    #[bpaf(long, short, argument("./.oxlintrc.json"))]
128	    pub config: Option<PathBuf>,
129	
130	    /// Override the TypeScript config used for import resolution.
131	    /// Oxlint automatically discovers the relevant `tsconfig.json` for each file.
132	    /// Use this only when your project uses a non-standard tsconfig name or location.
133	    ///
134	    /// ::: warning
135	    /// Avoid using this option. It can cause differences between import resolution,
136	    /// and type-aware linting. Type aware linting **does not** respect this option,
137	    /// and will always discover the appropriate `tsconfig.json` for each file automatically.
138	    /// :::
139	    #[bpaf(argument("./tsconfig.json"), hide_usage)]
140	    pub tsconfig: Option<PathBuf>,
141	
142	    /// Initialize oxlint configuration with default values
143	    #[bpaf(switch, hide_usage)]
144	    pub init: bool,
145	}
146	
147	// This is formatted according to
148	// <https://docs.rs/bpaf/latest/bpaf/params/struct.NamedArg.html#method.help>
149	//
150	/// Allowing / Denying Multiple Lints
151	///
152	/// Accumulate rules and categories from left to right on the command-line.
153	///   For example `-D correctness -A no-debugger` or `-A all -D no-debugger`.
154	///   The categories are:
155	///   * `correctness` - Code that is outright wrong or useless (default)
156	///   * `suspicious`  - Code that is most likely wrong or useless
157	///   * `pedantic`    - Lints which are rather strict or have occasional false positives
158	///   * `perf`        - Code that could be written in a more performant way
159	///   * `style`       - Code that should be written in a more idiomatic way
160	///   * `restriction` - Lints which prevent the use of language and library features
161	///   * `nursery`     - New lints that are still under development
162	///   * `all`         - All categories listed above except `nursery`. Does not enable plugins automatically.
163	///
164	/// Arguments:
165	//  ^ This shows up on the website but not from the cli's `--help`.
166	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Bpaf)]
167	pub enum LintFilter {
168	    Allow(
169	        /// Allow the rule or category (suppress the lint)
170	        #[bpaf(short('A'), long("allow"), argument("NAME"))]
171	        String,
172	    ),
173	    Warn(
174	        /// Deny the rule or category (emit a warning)
175	        #[bpaf(short('W'), long("warn"), argument("NAME"))]
176	        String,
177	    ),
178	    Deny(
179	        /// Deny the rule or category (emit an error)
180	        #[bpaf(short('D'), long("deny"), argument("NAME"))]
181	        String,
182	    ),
183	}
184	
185	impl LintFilter {
186	    fn into_tuple(self) -> (AllowWarnDeny, String) {
187	        match self {
188	            Self::Allow(s) => (AllowWarnDeny::Allow, s),
189	            Self::Warn(s) => (AllowWarnDeny::Warn, s),
190	            Self::Deny(s) => (AllowWarnDeny::Deny, s),
191	        }
192	    }
193	}
194	
195	/// Fix Problems
196	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Bpaf)]
197	pub struct FixOptions {
198	    /// Fix as many issues as possible. Only unfixed issues are reported in the output.
199	    #[bpaf(switch, hide_usage)]
200	    pub fix: bool,
201	    /// Apply auto-fixable suggestions. May change program behavior.
202	    #[bpaf(switch, hide_usage)]
203	    pub fix_suggestions: bool,
204	
205	    /// Apply dangerous fixes and suggestions
206	    #[bpaf(switch, hide_usage)]
207	    pub fix_dangerously: bool,
208	}
209	
210	impl FixOptions {
211	    pub fn fix_kind(&self) -> FixKind {
212	        let mut kind = FixKind::None;
213	
214	        if self.fix {
215	            kind.set(FixKind::SafeFix, true);
216	        }
217	
218	        if self.fix_suggestions {
219	            kind.set(FixKind::Suggestion, true);
220	        }
221	
222	        if self.fix_dangerously {
223	            if kind.is_none() {
224	                kind.set(FixKind::Fix, true);
225	            }
226	            kind.set(FixKind::Dangerous, true);
227	        }
228	
229	        kind
230	    }
231	
232	    pub fn is_enabled(&self) -> bool {
233	        self.fix || self.fix_suggestions || self.fix_dangerously
234	    }
235	}
236	
237	/// Handle Warnings
238	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Bpaf)]
239	pub struct WarningOptions {
240	    /// Disable reporting on warnings, only errors are reported

content.text: I found the formatter layer: `default` already implements `lint_command_info`, while `github` only reports diagnostic counts. Next I’m checking where that footer is emitted so the fix matches the existing flow.

content.text: I found the relevant formatter implementation: GitHub currently only customizes the diagnostic reporter and does not implement the `lint_command_info` hook that the default formatter uses for the “Finished in …” line. I’ll add a temporary unit test that reproduces this missing hook before fixing it.

content.text:
Implemented the fix for `--format=github` so it now prints the same lint summary footer as the default formatter.

Changes made:
- Refactored the default formatter’s “Finished in …” output into shared helper `get_lint_command_info_output`.
- Updated `GithubOutputFormatter` to implement `lint_command_info` and reuse that shared helper.
- Formatted with `cargo fmt --package oxlint`.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary targeted unit test to reproduce the missing GitHub formatter footer; confirmed it failed before the fix and passed after the fix.
- Per instructions, removed the temporary test afterward.
- Ran:
  - `cargo test -p oxlint output_formatter::github`
  - `cargo test -p oxlint output_formatter::default::test::lint_command_info`
- Manually verified:
  - `cargo run -p oxlint -- --format=github --silent apps/oxlint/fixtures/cli/output_formatter_diagnostic/ok.js`
  - Output now includes:
    - `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.`
    - `Finished in ... on 1 file ...`

Note:
- `just fmt` was unavailable in this environment (`just: command not found`), so I used `cargo fmt --package oxlint`.
- I did not commit changes.
- There were pre-existing unrelated modified snapshot files and `reports/` shown by `git status`; I did not touch or restore them.

result:
Implemented the fix for `--format=github` so it now prints the same lint summary footer as the default formatter.

Changes made:
- Refactored the default formatter’s “Finished in …” output into shared helper `get_lint_command_info_output`.
- Updated `GithubOutputFormatter` to implement `lint_command_info` and reuse that shared helper.
- Formatted with `cargo fmt --package oxlint`.

Validation performed:
- Added a temporary targeted unit test to reproduce the missing GitHub formatter footer; confirmed it failed before the fix and passed after the fix.
- Per instructions, removed the temporary test afterward.
- Ran:
  - `cargo test -p oxlint output_formatter::github`
  - `cargo test -p oxlint output_formatter::default::test::lint_command_info`
- Manually verified:
  - `cargo run -p oxlint -- --format=github --silent apps/oxlint/fixtures/cli/output_formatter_diagnostic/ok.js`
  - Output now includes:
    - `Found 0 warnings and 0 errors.`
    - `Finished in ... on 1 file ...`

Note:
- `just fmt` was unavailable in this environment (`just: command not found`), so I used `cargo fmt --package oxlint`.
- I did not commit changes.
- There were pre-existing unrelated modified snapshot files and `reports/` shown by `git status`; I did not touch or restore them.
